notes: Titanium diboride (TiB2) — ultra-hard ceramic with metallic conductivity. Density 4.52 g/cm³. Hardness ~2800-3000 HK (33 GPa). Flexural strength ~400 MPa. RHAe ~3.5-4.0 per mm vs KE — among the highest of any ceramic. Used in advanced armor concepts and composite armor arrays. Limited by cost, difficulty of sintering, and low fracture toughness K1c ~5-7 MPa·m^½. Not yet fielded in production vehicle armor.

source.reference: ARL/ceramics research literature, Campbell et al. (TiB2 armor), DTIC reports
source.methodology: RHAe estimated from ARL ballistic test data on TiB2 tiles vs AP threats, normalized to RHA thickness. Knoop hardness and fracture toughness from published materials science data.
